files.patch:
@@ -128,1 +128,1 @@ - JavaLabelOp, + StatementTargetOp, @@ -158,1 +158,20 @@ - * The lambda operation, that can model a Java lambda expression. + * The lambda operation, that can model Java language lambda expressions. + *

+ * Lambda operations are associated with a {@linkplain #functionalInterface() functional interface type}. + * They feature one body, the {@linkplain #body() function body}. + * The type of a lambda operation is its functional interface type. + *

+ * The function body takes as many arguments as the function type associated with the functional interface type. + * The function body yields a value if that function type has a non-{@linkplain JavaType#VOID void} return type. + *

+ * Lambda operations can also model Java language method reference expressions. A method reference is modelled as a + * lambda operation whose function body forwards its parameters to a corresponding {@link InvokeOp}, and that + * yields the result (if any) of that operation. + *

+ * Some lambda operations are reflectable (see {@link Reflect}), meaning their code model is persisted at + * runtime. + * + * @jls 15.27 Lambda Expressions + * @jls 15.13 Method Reference Expressions + * @jls 9.8 Functional Interfaces + * @jls 9.9 Function Types @@ -189,1 +208,1 @@ - * Builds a lambda operation with the provided function body. + * Completes the lambda operation by adding the function body. @@ -191,1 +210,1 @@ - * @param c a consumer to populate the lambda function body + * @param c a consumer that populates the function body @@ -201,1 +220,2 @@ - * Marks the constructed lambda operation as reflectable. + * Returns a builder that constructs a reflectable lambda operation. + * @@ -259,1 +279,1 @@ - * {@return the functional interface type modeled by this lambda operation} + * {@return the functional interface type modelled by this lambda operation} @@ -491,0 +511,4 @@ + *

+ * Throw operations feature one operand, the value being thrown. + * + * @jls 14.18 The throw Statement @@ -532,1 +556,10 @@ - * The assertion operation. Supporting assertions in statement form. + * The assertion operation, that can model Java language assert statements. + *

+ * Assert operations feature one or two bodies. The first body, called the predicate body, models the + * assertion condition. If present, the second body, called the details body, models the detail + * expression. + *

+ * The predicate body should accept no arguments and yield a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * If present, the details body should accept no arguments and yield a value. + * + * @jls 14.10 The assert Statement @@ -660,0 +693,11 @@ + *

+ * The method invoked by an invoke operation is specified using a + * {@linkplain MethodRef method reference}. + * The operands of an invoke operation are specified as follows: + *

    + *
  • For {@linkplain InvokeKind#STATIC static} invocations, operands are the invocation arguments.
    • + *
  • For {@linkplain InvokeKind#INSTANCE instance} and {@linkplain InvokeKind#SUPER super} invocations, the first + * operand is the receiver and the remaining operands are the invocation arguments.
    • + *
+ * + * @jls 15.12 Method Invocation Expressions @@ -851,0 +895,6 @@ + *

+ * Conversion operations feature one operand, the value to convert. + * + * @jls 15.16 Cast Expressions + * @jls 5.1.2 Widening Primitive Conversion + * @jls 5.1.3 Narrowing Primitive Conversion @@ -887,1 +937,8 @@ - * The new operation, that can models Java language instance creation expressions. + * The new operation, that can model Java language instance creation expressions and array creation expressions. + *

+ * The constructor invoked by a new operation is specified using a + * {@linkplain MethodRef constructor reference}. + * New operations feature operands corresponding to the constructor arguments. + * + * @jls 15.9 Class Instance Creation Expressions + * @jls 15.10.1 Array Creation Expressions @@ -1001,1 +1058,8 @@ - * A field access operation, that can model Java langauge field access expressions. + * A field access operation, that can model Java language field access expressions. + *

+ * The field accessed by a field access operation is specified using a {@linkplain FieldRef field + * reference}. + *

+ * Instance field accesses feature a receiver operand. Static field accesses have no receiver operand. + * + * @jls 15.11 Field Access Expressions @@ -1038,2 +1102,3 @@ - * The field load operation, that can model Java language field access expressions combined with load access to - * field instance variables. + * The field load operation, that can model Java language field access expressions used to read a field value. + * + * @jls 15.11 Field Access Expressions @@ -1097,2 +1162,5 @@ - * The field store operation, that can model Java language field access expressions combined with store access - * to field instance variables. + * The field store operation, that can model Java language field access expressions used to write a field value. + *

+ * The result type is always {@link JavaType#VOID}. + * + * @jls 15.11 Field Access Expressions @@ -1149,0 +1217,5 @@ + *

+ * Array length operations feature one operand, the array value. + * The type of an array length operation is {@link JavaType#INT}. + * + * @jls 15.11 Field Access Expressions @@ -1180,0 +1253,5 @@ + *

+ * Array access operations feature two operands, the array value and the index value. + * Array store operations feature an additional operand, the stored value. + * + * @jls 15.10.3 Array Access Expressions @@ -1201,0 +1279,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.10.3 Array Access Expressions @@ -1245,0 +1325,4 @@ + *

+ * The type of an array store operation is {@link JavaType#VOID}. + * + * @jls 15.10.3 Array Access Expressions @@ -1280,2 +1364,7 @@ - * The instanceof operation, that can model Java language instanceof expressions when the instanceof keyword is a - * type comparison operator. + * The instanceof operation, that can model Java language instanceof expressions that use the + * {@code instanceof} keyword as the type comparison operator. + *

+ * Instanceof operations feature one operand, the value being tested, and are associated with a + * {@linkplain JavaType type} modelling the target type of the type comparison operator. + * + * @jls 15.20.2 The instanceof Operator @@ -1343,0 +1432,5 @@ + *

+ * Cast operations feature one operand, the value being cast, and are associated with a + * {@linkplain JavaType type} modelling the target type of the cast. + * + * @jls 15.16 Cast Expressions @@ -1532,1 +1626,7 @@ - * The String Concatenation Operation + * The string concatenation operation, that can model the Java language string concatenation operator + * {@code +}. + *

+ * Concatenation operations feature two operands. + * The type of a string concatenation operation is {@linkplain JavaType#J_L_STRING java.lang.String}. + * + * @jls 15.18.1 String Concatenation Operator + @@ -1596,1 +1696,5 @@ - * The binary arithmetic operation. + * A binary arithmetic operation. + *

+ * Binary arithmetic operations feature two operands. Usually, both operands have the same type, + * although that is not always the case. The type of a binary arithmetic operation is + * the type of the first operand. @@ -1615,0 +1719,3 @@ + *

+ * Unary arithmetic operations feature one operand. + * The result type of a unary arithmetic operation is the type of its operand. @@ -1633,0 +1740,2 @@ + *

+ * Binary test operations feature two operands, and yield a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. @@ -1651,0 +1760,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.18.2 Additive Operators (+ and -) for Numeric Types @@ -1676,0 +1787,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.18.2 Additive Operators (+ and -) for Numeric Types @@ -1701,0 +1814,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.17.1 Multiplication Operator * @@ -1726,0 +1841,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.17.2 Division Operator / @@ -1751,0 +1868,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.17.3 Remainder Operator % @@ -1777,0 +1896,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.22 Bitwise and Logical Operators @@ -1803,0 +1924,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.22 Bitwise and Logical Operators @@ -1829,0 +1952,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.22 Bitwise and Logical Operators @@ -1854,0 +1979,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.19 Shift Operators @@ -1879,0 +2006,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.19 Shift Operators @@ -1904,0 +2033,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.19 Shift Operators @@ -1929,0 +2060,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.15.4 Unary Minus Operator {@code -} @@ -1954,0 +2087,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.15.5 Bitwise Complement Operator {@code ~} @@ -1979,0 +2114,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.15.6 Logical Complement Operator {@code !} @@ -2005,0 +2142,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.21 Equality Operators @@ -2031,0 +2170,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.21 Equality Operators @@ -2056,0 +2197,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.20.1 Numerical Comparison Operators {@code <}, {@code <=}, {@code >}, and {@code >=} @@ -2082,0 +2225,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.20.1 Numerical Comparison Operators {@code <}, {@code <=}, {@code >}, and {@code >=} @@ -2108,0 +2253,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.20.1 Numerical Comparison Operators {@code <}, {@code <=}, {@code >}, and {@code >=} @@ -2134,0 +2281,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.20.1 Numerical Comparison Operators {@code <}, {@code <=}, {@code >}, and {@code >=} @@ -2158,1 +2307,7 @@ - * The label operation, that can model Java language statements with label identifiers. + * A statement target operation, that can model Java language statements associated with label identifiers. + *

+ * Statement target operations feature zero or one operand, the label identifier. + * If present, the label identifier is modelled as a {@link ConstantOp} value. + * + * @jls 14.15 The break Statement + * @jls 14.16 The continue Statement @@ -2160,1 +2315,1 @@ - public sealed static abstract class JavaLabelOp extends JavaOp + public sealed static abstract class StatementTargetOp extends JavaOp @@ -2162,1 +2317,1 @@ - JavaLabelOp(JavaLabelOp that, CodeContext cc) { + StatementTargetOp(StatementTargetOp that, CodeContext cc) { @@ -2166,1 +2321,1 @@ - JavaLabelOp(Value label) { + StatementTargetOp(Value label) { @@ -2242,1 +2397,5 @@ - * The break operation, that can model Java language break statements with label identifiers. + * The break operation, that can model Java language break statements. + *

+ * Break operations feature zero or one operand, the label identifier. + * + * @jls 14.15 The break Statement @@ -2245,1 +2404,1 @@ - public static final class BreakOp extends JavaLabelOp { + public static final class BreakOp extends StatementTargetOp { @@ -2272,1 +2431,5 @@ - * The continue operation, that can model Java language continue statements with label identifiers. + * The continue operation, that can model Java language continue statements. + *

+ * Continue operations feature zero or one operand, the label identifier. + * + * @jls 14.16 The continue Statement @@ -2275,1 +2438,1 @@ - public static final class ContinueOp extends JavaLabelOp { + public static final class ContinueOp extends StatementTargetOp { @@ -2303,0 +2466,4 @@ + *

+ * Yield operations feature one operand, the yielded value. + * + * @jls 14.21 The yield Statement @@ -2310,2 +2477,2 @@ - if (def.operands().size() > 1) { - throw new IllegalArgumentException("Operation must have zero or one operand " + def.name()); + if (def.operands().size() != 1) { + throw new IllegalArgumentException("Operation must have one operand " + def.name()); @@ -2314,1 +2481,1 @@ - this(def.operands().isEmpty() ? null : def.operands().get(0)); + this(def.operands().get(0)); @@ -2327,1 +2494,1 @@ - super(operand == null ? List.of() : List.of(operand)); + super(List.of(Objects.requireNonNull(operand))); @@ -2334,6 +2501,1 @@ - if (operands().size() == 1) { - return operands().get(0); - } else { - // @@@ - return null; - } + return operands().get(0); @@ -2384,0 +2546,5 @@ + *

+ * Block operations feature one statements body, modelling the list of statements enclosed by the Java block. + * The statements body should accept no arguments and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.2 Blocks @@ -2461,0 +2628,7 @@ + *

+ * Synchronized operations feature two bodies. The expression body accepts no arguments + * and yields a value, the object associated with the monitor that will be acquired by the synchronized + * operation. The block body models the statements to execute while holding the monitor, + * and yields {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.19 The synchronized Statement @@ -2487,0 +2661,1 @@ + // @@@: builder? @@ -2546,1 +2721,1 @@ - (op instanceof JavaOp.JavaLabelOp lop && ifExitFromSynchronized(lop))) { + (op instanceof StatementTargetOp lop && ifExitFromSynchronized(lop))) { @@ -2602,1 +2777,1 @@ - boolean ifExitFromSynchronized(JavaLabelOp lop) { + boolean ifExitFromSynchronized(StatementTargetOp lop) { @@ -2615,0 +2790,8 @@ + *

+ * Labeled operations feature one body, the labeled body. The labeled body accepts no arguments and + * yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + *

+ * The entry block of the labeled body always begins with a {@linkplain ConstantOp} constant modelling + * the label associated with the labeled statement, followed by the statement being labeled. + * + * @jls 14.7 Labeled Statements @@ -2705,1 +2888,13 @@ - * The if operation, that can model Java language if, if-then, and if-then-else statements. + * The if operation, that can model Java language if statements. + *

+ * If operations feature multiple bodies. Some bodies, called predicate bodies, model conditions that + * determine which execution path the evaluation of the if operation should take. Other bodies, called + * action bodies, model the statements to be executed when the preceding predicate is satisfied. + *

+ * Each predicate body has a corresponding action body, and there may be a trailing action body with no + * predicate, modelling the code after the Java {@code else} keyword. + *

+ * Predicate bodies should accept no arguments and yield a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * Action bodies similarly accept no arguments, and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.9 The if Statement @@ -2716,1 +2911,1 @@ - * Builder for the initial condition branch of an if operation. + * Builder for the initial predicate body of an if operation. @@ -2728,3 +2923,4 @@ - * Begins an if-branch with the given predicate builder. - * @param c a consumer for the predicate body - * @return a then-branch builder to complete the if operation + * Begins an if operation by adding the initial predicate body. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body + * @return a builder to add an action body to the if operation @@ -2742,1 +2938,1 @@ - * Builder for the then-branch of an if operation. + * Builder for the action body of an if operation. @@ -2754,3 +2950,4 @@ - * Adds a then-branch with the given body. - * @param c a consumer for the then-branch body - * @return a builder for further else-if-/else-branches + * Adds an action body to the if operation. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the action body + * @return a builder for further predicate and action bodies @@ -2767,2 +2964,2 @@ - * Adds a then-branch with an empty body. - * @return a builder for further else-if-/else-branches + * Adds an empty action body to the if operation. + * @return a builder for further predicate and action bodies @@ -2780,1 +2977,1 @@ - * Builder for additional else-if-/else-branches of an if operation. + * Builder for additional predicate and action bodies of an if operation. @@ -2792,3 +2989,4 @@ - * Adds an else-if-branch to the if operation. - * @param c a consumer for the else-if predicate - * @return a then-branch builder for the else-if body + * Adds a predicate body to the if operation. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body + * @return a builder to add an action body to the if operation @@ -2805,2 +3003,3 @@ - * Adds an else-branch with the given body. - * @param c a consumer for the else-branch body + * Completes the if operation by adding the final action body. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the action body @@ -2818,1 +3017,1 @@ - * Adds an else-branch with an empty body. + * Complete the if operation with an empty action body. @@ -2957,0 +3156,11 @@ + *

+ * Switch operations are parameterized by a selector value. + * They feature a sequence of case bodies, each modelled as a pair of bodies: a predicate body and an + * action body. + *

+ * Each predicate body accepts one argument, the selector value, and yields a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * Each action body yields a value of the same type {@code T}. For switch statement operations, {@code T} is + * {@code void}. For switch expression operations, {@code T} is the switch expression type. + * + * @jls 14.11 The switch Statement + * @jls 15.28 {@code switch} Expressions @@ -2977,1 +3187,1 @@ - // The statements body has no parameters and returns void + // The action body has no parameters and returns void @@ -3114,1 +3324,1 @@ - case null is modeled like this: + case null is modelled like this: @@ -3142,0 +3352,5 @@ + *

+ * For switch expression operations, action bodies yield a value of type {@code T}, where {@code T} is also the + * type of the switch expression operation. + * + * @jls 15.28 {@code switch} Expressions @@ -3179,0 +3394,4 @@ + *

+ * For switch statement operations, action bodies yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.11 The switch Statement @@ -3256,1 +3475,15 @@ - * The for operation, that can model a Java language for statement. + * The for operation, that can model a Java language basic for statement. + *

+ * For operations feature four bodies that model a basic {@code for} statement: + * an initialization body, a predicate body, an update body, and a loop body. + *

+ * The initialization body accepts no arguments and yields the loop state, of type {@code S}. For instance, + * a loop with a single loop variable of type {@code T} might use a loop state of type {@code T}. + * A loop with two loop variables of type {@code X} and {@code Y} might use a loop state whose type is + * a {@linkplain TupleType tuple type}, such as {@code (X, Y)}. A loop with no loop variables might use + * a loop state of type {@link JavaType#VOID}, and have its initialization body yield no value. + *

+ * The predicate body accepts an argument of type {@code S} and yields a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * The update and loop bodies accept an argument of type {@code S} and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.14.1 The basic for Statement @@ -3263,1 +3496,1 @@ - * Builder for the initialization phase of a for-loop. + * Builder for the initialization body of a for operation. @@ -3276,3 +3509,4 @@ - * Builds the initialization step for a for-loop. - * @param c a consumer to model the code initializing the loop variables - * @return a conditional builder for the loop condition + * Builds the initialization body of a for-loop. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the initialization body + * @return a builder for specifying the loop predicate body @@ -3290,1 +3524,1 @@ - * Builder for the condition (predicate) phase of a for-loop. + * Builder for the predicate body of a for operation. @@ -3306,3 +3540,4 @@ - * Builds the predicate body for the loop. - * @param c a consumer to model the for-loop condition - * @return an update builder for building the update phase + * Builds the predicate body of a for-loop. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body + * @return a builder for specifying the update body @@ -3320,1 +3555,1 @@ - * Builder for the update phase of a for-loop (executed after each iteration). + * Builder for the update body of a for operation. @@ -3338,3 +3573,4 @@ - * Builds the update logic to be run after each for-loop iteration. - * @param c a consumer for the update logic - * @return a body builder for constructing the loop body + * Builds the update body of a for-loop. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the update body + * @return a builder for specifying the loop body @@ -3342,1 +3578,1 @@ - public ForOp.BodyBuilder cond(Consumer c) { + public ForOp.BodyBuilder update(Consumer c) { @@ -3372,2 +3608,3 @@ - * Builds the loop body for the for-loop. - * @param c a consumer for the main logic to run in the for-loop + * Completes for operation by adding the loop body. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the loop body @@ -3538,0 +3775,11 @@ + *

+ * Enhanced-for operations feature three bodies. The expression body models the expression to be + * iterated. The definition body models the definition of the loop variable. The loop body + * models the statements to execute. + *

+ * The expression body accepts no arguments and yields a value of type {@code I}, corresponding to the type of the + * expression to be iterated. The definition body accepts one argument of type {@code E}, corresponding to an element + * type derived from {@code I}, and yields a value of type {@code V}, the type of the loop variable. Finally, the loop + * body accepts that value and yields {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.14.2 The enhanced for statement @@ -3544,1 +3792,1 @@ - * Builder for the iterable expression in an enhanced-for loop. + * Builder for the expression body of an enhanced-for operation. @@ -3559,3 +3807,4 @@ - * Builds the iterable expression for an enhanced-for loop. - * @param c a consumer that populates the block modelling the iterable expression - * @return a definition builder, for specifying the variable definition in the loop + * Builds the expression body of an enhanced-for operation. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the expression body + * @return a builder for specifying the definition body @@ -3573,1 +3822,1 @@ - * Builder for the iteration variable definition in an enhanced-for loop. + * Builder for the definition body of an enhanced-for operation. @@ -3588,4 +3837,5 @@ - * Defines the loop variable for the enhanced-for operation, using a type - * derived from the iterable expression. - * @param c a consumer that populates the block defining the loop variable - * @return a body builder for the loop body + * Builds the definition body of an enhanced-for operation, using a type derived from the type + * of the loop expression. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the definition body + * @return a builder for specifying the loop body @@ -3598,4 +3848,5 @@ - * Defines the loop variable for the enhanced-for operation with the provided type. - * @param bodyElementType the type to use for the variable in the loop body - * @param c a consumer that populates the block defining the loop variable - * @return a body builder for the loop body + * Builds the definition body of an enhanced-for operation with the provided type. + * + * @param bodyElementType the type to provide to the loop body + * @param c a consumer that populates the definition body + * @return a builder for specifying the loop body @@ -3613,1 +3864,1 @@ - * Builder for an enhanced-for operation body. + * Builder for the loop body of an enhanced-for operation. @@ -3630,2 +3881,3 @@ - * Completes the construction of the enhanced-for loop operation with the body obtained from the provided builder. - * @param c a consumer that populates the block for the enhanced-for loop body + * Completes the enhanced-for operation by adding the loop body. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the loop body @@ -3810,0 +4062,8 @@ + *

+ * While operations feature two bodies. The predicate body models the loop condition. + * The loop body models the statements to execute. + *

+ * The predicate body should accept no arguments and yield a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * The loop body should accept no arguments, and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.12 The while Statement @@ -3816,1 +4076,1 @@ - * A builder for specifying the predicate (loop condition) of a while loop operation. + * Builder for the predicate body of a while operation. @@ -3826,1 +4086,1 @@ - * Builds the predicate (loop condition) body for the while operation. + * Builds the predicate body of a while operation. @@ -3828,2 +4088,2 @@ - * @param c a consumer to populate the block for the predicate - * @return a builder for the main loop body + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body + * @return a builder for specifying the loop body @@ -3840,1 +4100,1 @@ - * A builder for specifying the main body of a while loop operation. + * Builder for the loop body of a while operation. @@ -3852,1 +4112,1 @@ - * Builds the main logic body for the while loop operation. + * Completes the while operation by adding the loop body. @@ -3854,2 +4114,2 @@ - * @param c a consumer to populate the block for the loop body - * @return the completed while loop operation + * @param c a consumer that populates the loop body + * @return the completed while operation @@ -3962,0 +4222,8 @@ + *

+ * Do-while operations feature two bodies. The loop body models the statements to execute. + * The predicate body models the loop condition. + *

+ * The loop body should accept no arguments, and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. The predicate body + * should accept no arguments, and yield a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * + * @jls 14.13 The do Statement @@ -3969,1 +4237,1 @@ - * Builder for a do-while predicate. + * Builder for the predicate body of a do-while operation. @@ -3981,3 +4249,4 @@ - * Completes the construction of a do-while operation with the predicate obtained from the provided builder. - * @param c a consumer that populates the block modelling the do-while predicate - * @return a completed do-while operation with the provided predicate + * Completes the do-while operation by adding the predicate body. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body + * @return the completed do-while operation @@ -3994,1 +4263,1 @@ - * Builder for a do-while body. + * Builder for the loop body of a do-while operation. @@ -4004,3 +4273,4 @@ - * Builds the body of a do-while loop. - * @param c a consumer that populates the block modeling the body of the loop - * @return a predicate builder for configuring this do-while operation + * Builds the loop body of a do-while operation. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the loop body + * @return a builder for specifying the predicate body @@ -4111,1 +4381,6 @@ - * The conditional-and-or operation, that can model Java language condition-or or conditional-and expressions. + * The conditional operation, that can model Java language conditional-and and conditional-or expressions. + *

+ * Conditional operations feature two or more predicate bodies, each yielding a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * + * @jls 15.23 Conditional-And Operator {@code &&} + * @jls 15.24 Conditional-Or Operator {@code ||} @@ -4205,0 +4480,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.23 Conditional-And Operator {@code &&} @@ -4224,2 +4501,3 @@ - * Adds a condition to this builder. - * @param c the consumer populating the block modelling the additional condition + * Adds a predicate body to this conditional-and operation. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body @@ -4237,1 +4515,1 @@ - * {@return the conditional-and operation from the accumulated conditions} + * {@return the completed conditional-and operation} @@ -4271,0 +4549,2 @@ + * + * @jls 15.24 Conditional-Or Operator {@code ||} @@ -4290,2 +4570,3 @@ - * Adds a condition to this builder. - * @param c the consumer populating the block modelling the additional condition + * Adds a predicate body to this conditional-or operation. + * + * @param c a consumer that populates the predicate body @@ -4303,1 +4584,1 @@ - * {@return the conditional-or operation from the accumulated conditions} + * {@return the completed conditional-or operation} @@ -4337,0 +4618,7 @@ + *

+ * Conditional expression operations feature three bodies: the predicate body, the true body, and the false body. + *

+ * The predicate body accepts no arguments and yields a {@link JavaType#BOOLEAN} value. + * The true and false bodies accepts no arguments and yield a value. + * + * @jls 15.25 Conditional Operator {@code ? :} @@ -4432,0 +4720,17 @@ + *

+ * Try operations feature a try body, zero or more catch bodies, and an optional + * finalizer body. Try operations may also feature an optional resources body, modelling a + * try-with-resources statement. + *

+ * The resources body, if present, accepts no arguments and yields a value of type {@code R}. + * For instance, a try-with-resources statement with two resources of type {@code X} and {@code Y}, + * {@code R} is a {@linkplain TupleType tuple type}, such as {@code (X, Y)}. + *

+ * The try body yields {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. If a resources body is present then the try body should + * accept an argument of type {@code R}, otherwise it accepts no arguments. + *

+ * Each catch body should accept an exception value and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. The + * finalizer body, if present, should accept no arguments and yield {@linkplain JavaType#VOID no value}. + * + * @jls 14.20 The try statement + * @jls 14.20.3 The try-with-resources statement @@ -4438,1 +4743,1 @@ -
